LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST SPECIFICALLY STATE ITS NUMBER, ITS NATURE, AND THE PARTY OR PARTIES TO WHOM IT IS DIRECTED. IT IS NOT POSSIBLE TO IDENTIFY FROM THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT WHICH ALLEGATIONS PERTAIN TO WHICH DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS TO SUPPORT EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF NANCY WOLD'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ORDER GRANTING DISMISSAL OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 473.

APPEARANCES: Geoffrey Brethen, Esq. of Adorno Yoss Alvarado & Smith 714/852-6800 for deft & opposing party California Reconveyance Company (appearing via CourtCall)[appearing for another counsel & unable to determine if proper notice was given]; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANTED. CCP SECTION 473(B). FURTHERMORE, THE CLERK'S OFFICE WILL NOT PERMIT THE FILING OF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT UNTIL AN ORDER SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER AND GRANTING LEAVE TO AMEND HAS BEEN FILED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc And Motion To Dismiss Complaint For Failure To Amend
Off Calendar Jul-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Valerie Brennan of Adorno Yoss Alvarado & Smith (714.852.6800) appearing via Court Call for defendant and moving party
No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT FOR FAILURE TO AMEND granted, no opposition filed. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-14-2010 Continued To Aug-20-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Jenny Merris, Esq. of Adorno, Yoss, Alvarado & Smith (714) 852-6800 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendant and Moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to each cause of action, Plaintiff must specifically state its number, its nature, and the party or parties to whom it is directed. It is not possible to identify from the face of the complaint which allegations pertain to which defendant. Plaintiff must allege facts to support each cause of action. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Katy Anne Bersamin, CSR # 11722
